settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.subtitle: System configuration and integrations
settingsPage.logForwardingHint: Syslog, Kafka, Webhook destinations are configured via the Log Forwarding page.

contentFilter.title: Content Filter Rules
contentFilter.intro: Each AI request is checked against these rules. When the user message matches a rule, the gateway runs the rule's action: Block rejects the request, Warn allows it but logs an alert, Log silently records the hit. Changes apply immediately, no restart needed.
contentFilter.empty: No rules yet. Add rules manually or load a preset to get started.
contentFilter.actionBlockHint: Reject the request with an error.
contentFilter.actionWarnHint: Allow the request, raise a warning in audit logs.
contentFilter.actionLogHint: Allow the request silently, only record in logs.
contentFilter.sandboxDesc: Paste a sample user message to see which of your current (unsaved) rules would fire and which action would run.
contentFilter.sandboxPlaceholder: Type or paste sample text here…
contentFilter.presetsDesc: Click a preset to append its rules to your current list. Existing rules are kept. You can edit each rule afterward.

basic.name: Basic defense
basic.description: Block the most common jailbreak and instruction-override patterns. Recommended starting point.

strict.name: Strict defense
strict.description: Adds persona manipulation, prompt extraction, and Base64 smuggling detection on top of the basic ruleset.

chinese.name: Chinese language
chinese.description: Common Chinese-language prompt injection patterns: 越狱, 忽略指令, 扮演 …

pii.title: PII Redactor
pii.intro: These regex patterns are matched against user messages before they are forwarded to the AI provider. Each match is replaced with a placeholder; the AI's response is then post-processed to restore the original values, so the upstream model never sees the raw PII.
pii.empty: No PII patterns configured. Add a pattern to start redacting.
pii.name: Name
pii.behavior: Each pattern: 1) finds matches in user input, 2) replaces them with {{PREFIX_xxxx_n}} placeholders before sending upstream, 3) restores original values in the response. Changes apply immediately, no restart needed.
pii.sandboxDesc: Paste sample text to see how it would look after the current (unsaved) PII patterns are applied.
pii.sandboxPlaceholder: Type or paste sample text containing emails, phone numbers, etc.

retention.title: Data Retention
retention.intro: How long to keep records before automatic deletion. Usage records live in PostgreSQL; the six log types live in ClickHouse and are cleaned up by ClickHouse TTL. Saving applies the new TTL to the corresponding table immediately — old data outside the new window is purged in the background.
retention.usageHint: Per-request token / cost records used by analytics.
retention.auditHint: Security audit trail (login, settings, key ops).
retention.gatewayHint: AI API request logs (model, tokens, cost).
retention.accessHint: HTTP access log for both ports.

settings.publicGatewayUrlHint: How the gateway is reached from the outside world. Used in the Configuration Guide and copy-paste examples. Leave fields empty / 0 to auto-detect from the browser.
settings.allowRegistrationHint: When enabled, anyone can create an account through the registration page.
settings.clientIpSourceHint: How to determine the client's real IP address.

guide.title: Configuration Guide
guide.subtitle: Configure your AI tools and MCP clients to use ThinkWatch
guide.gatewayUrlDesc: AI Gateway endpoint for OpenAI / Anthropic compatible requests
guide.mcpEndpointDesc: MCP Gateway endpoint for Streamable HTTP tool invocations
guide.aiGatewayDesc: Connect AI coding tools and SDKs to ThinkWatch as an OpenAI / Anthropic proxy
guide.mcpGatewayDesc: Connect MCP-compatible clients to access tools registered in ThinkWatch
guide.mcpPromptDesc: Copy the prompt below and send it to your AI assistant — it will detect your MCP client and walk you through the configuration.
guide.mcpPromptTemplate:
I need to configure my AI tool's MCP (Model Context Protocol) client to connect to an MCP gateway called ThinkWatch.

Here is the information you need:

MCP Endpoint: {{mcpUrl}}/mcp
Transport: Streamable HTTP (protocol version 2025-03-26)
API Key: (ask the user, it starts with "tw-")

The MCP server configuration should be:
{
  "type": "streamableHttp",
  "url": "{{mcpUrl}}/mcp",
  "headers": {
    "Authorization": "Bearer <API_KEY>"
  }
}

Configuration for specific tools:
- Claude Desktop: add to mcpServers in claude_desktop_config.json (macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json, Windows: %APPDATA%\\Claude\\claude_desktop_config.json)
- Claude Code CLI: run `claude mcp add think-watch --transport streamable-http "{{mcpUrl}}/mcp" --header "Authorization: Bearer <key>"`
- Cursor: add to mcpServers in .cursor/mcp.json (project) or ~/.cursor/mcp.json (global)
- Cline: add to mcpServers in cline_mcp_settings.json or via Cline Settings > MCP Servers
- VS Code / Copilot: add to mcpServers in .vscode/mcp.json

Please detect which tool I am using and help me configure it step by step.

promptStep.paste: Paste it into your AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.)
promptStep.follow: Follow the AI's instructions to complete the configuration

guide.promptTip: The prompt already includes your gateway URL. The AI assistant will ask for your API Key and generate the configuration automatically.
guide.claudeDesktopDesc: Configure Claude Desktop to use MCP tools through ThinkWatch.
guide.claudeCodeMcpDesc: Register ThinkWatch as an MCP server directly from the Claude Code CLI.
guide.cursorMcpDesc: Configure Cursor IDE to connect to ThinkWatch MCP tools via mcp.json.
guide.clineMcpDesc: Configure Cline VS Code extension to use ThinkWatch MCP tools.
guide.mcpSdkDesc: Use the official MCP SDK to integrate ThinkWatch tools into your application.
guide.mcpCurlDesc: Test the MCP gateway directly with cURL using JSON-RPC over Streamable HTTP.
guide.mcpProtocolDesc: ThinkWatch supports the MCP Streamable HTTP transport (2025-03-26 spec)
guide.mcpVersionNote: Implements MCP protocol version 2025-03-26 with Streamable HTTP transport
guide.mcpSessionIdNote: Save the Mcp-Session-Id from the initialization response and include it in subsequent requests

mcpStep.openConfig: Open the Claude Desktop configuration file
mcpStep.addServer: Add an mcpServers entry for ThinkWatch
mcpStep.setApiKey: Set the Authorization header with your tw- prefixed API key
mcpStep.restartApp: Restart the application to load the new MCP server
mcpStep.runAdd: Run claude mcp add with the Streamable HTTP transport
mcpStep.verifyList: Verify with claude mcp list that the server is registered
mcpStep.useTools: Start using MCP tools in Claude Code — they appear automatically
mcpStep.addServerEntry: Add an mcpServers entry with type streamableHttp
mcpStep.openClineSettings: Open Cline settings in VS Code and navigate to MCP Servers
mcpStep.toolsAppear: MCP tools will appear automatically in Cline's tool list

guide.claudeCodeDesc: Claude Code connects using the Anthropic native format. Set environment variables to route all requests through ThinkWatch.
guide.cursorDesc: Cursor uses the OpenAI-compatible API format. Configure it in the editor settings.
guide.cursorEndpointNote: Endpoint: /v1/chat/completions (OpenAI-compatible). Supports all models routed through ThinkWatch.
guide.continueDesc: Continue connects as an OpenAI-compatible provider. Edit the config file to point at ThinkWatch.
guide.clineDesc: Cline supports OpenAI-compatible providers. Configure it through the extension settings.
guide.openaiSdkDesc: Use the official OpenAI Python SDK with ThinkWatch as a drop-in replacement.
guide.anthropicSdkDesc: Use the official Anthropic Python SDK by pointing it at ThinkWatch.
guide.anthropicSdkNote: The Anthropic SDK uses the base URL without the /v1 suffix, unlike OpenAI-compatible clients.
guide.curlDesc: Test the gateway directly with cURL. Both OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic message formats are supported.

aiStep.createKey: Create an API key in AI Gateway > API Keys (it starts with tw-)
aiStep.claudeCodeBaseUrl: Set ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to your ThinkWatch gateway URL
aiStep.claudeCodeApiKey: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the key you just created
aiStep.claudeCodeRun: Run claude as usual — all requests are proxied through the gateway
aiStep.cursorOpen: Open Cursor Settings and navigate to Models > OpenAI API Key
aiStep.cursorApiKey: Paste the API key you just created into the API Key field
aiStep.cursorSelectModel: Select any model configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.continueOpen: Open ~/.continue/config.json (or use the Continue settings UI)
aiStep.continueAdd: Add a new model entry with provider set to "openai"
aiStep.continueApiKey: Set apiKey to the key you just created
aiStep.continueChooseModel: Choose any model name configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.clineOpen: Open Cline Settings in VS Code and select API Provider > OpenAI Compatible
aiStep.clineApiKey: Paste the API key you just created into the API Key field
aiStep.clineModel: Enter a model name (e.g. gpt-4o) configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.openaiSdkInstall: Install the OpenAI SDK: pip install openai
aiStep.openaiSdkApiKey: Set api_key to the key you just created
aiStep.openaiSdkModel: Use any model configured in ThinkWatch
aiStep.anthropicSdkInstall: Install the Anthropic SDK: pip install anthropic
aiStep.anthropicSdkApiKey: Set api_key to the key you just created
aiStep.anthropicSdkModel: Use any Claude model configured in ThinkWatch
